refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: supporting teleoperation with a specified communication quality metric (packet loss limit). It does not combine multiple requirements and avoids specifying how the communication quality is achieved, thus remaining solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit during teleoperation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to ensure packet loss stays below threshold
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and error correction algorithms
Interaction between retransmission logic and teleoperation control timing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality management into a protocol layer that can be independently designed and adjusted. It minimizes coupling by focusing on adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms rather than physical channel constraints.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and stable enough for adaptive protocols to be effective
Risk that retransmission delays may impact teleoperation responsiveness if not carefully managed
Assumes that error correction overhead does not excessively increase latency or bandwidth usage
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including latency and jitter constraints alongside packet loss in the FR to ensure comprehensive communication quality coverage.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core communication and networking functionalities, including protocol implementation and adaptive error correction mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional interface for sending and receiving data packets over the network, essential for implementing communication protocols.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs communication status and performance metrics related to packet loss and retransmission.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol with adaptive error correction is central to NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion as most related functions reside here.
Low containment; Vehicle module likely uses communication but does not implement protocol logic. High diffusion if protocol implemented here.
Some involvement in communication for remote control, but protocol implementation is not primary responsibility, leading to moderate diffusion.
Safety Services may monitor communication health but does not implement protocol, resulting in poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the communication protocol with adaptive error correction, as it centralizes networking functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if it requires specialized communication handling.
Risk of coupling with Vehicle module if vehicle-specific adaptations are needed.
Interface complexity on Network Link may require careful management to avoid leakage of protocol details.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission to meet the packet loss limit, aligning well with the customer-defined quality requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if specialized communication handling is required.
Risk of coupling with Vehicle module if vehicle-specific adaptations are needed.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well centralized within this IM. Other modules have low containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics output via the Telemetry interface, which reports communication status and performance related to packet loss and retransmission.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation is clear as the NGHE Solution module encompasses the core communication functionalities required to realize the FR, with well-defined external interfaces for network communication and telemetry.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface management on the Network Link to prevent leakage of protocol details and maintain modularity.
Assess and document any specialized communication handling needs from the Teleoperation module to avoid diffusion risks.
Evaluate potential coupling with the Vehicle module and define clear boundaries or adaptation layers if vehicle-specific protocol modifications are necessary.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and high containment of the DP within the NGHE Solution module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the DP directly addresses the FR with an adaptive communication protocol meeting the packet loss limit. Although some diffusion risks and interface management clarifications are noted, these are manageable and do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and centralized implementation boundary is preferable to avoid complexity and potential errors.

revise.why: Clarify interface management on the Network Link to prevent leakage of protocol details and maintain modularity, and assess specialized communication handling needs from Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to mitigate diffusion risks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate the communication protocol effectively.
Telemetry interface provides reliable packet loss metrics for verification.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module requiring specialized handling.
Risk of coupling with Vehicle module if vehicle-specific adaptations are needed.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface management on Network Link.
Assessment reports on Teleoperation and Vehicle module communication handling requirements.
